inline_comments.message:
hey I was just thinking why cant we directly use the cps-service methods ?? 
Like directly injecting CpsAdminService , CpsModuleService ?? etc..
Do we already thought about it and had some problems around it ??
hmm i did try a way to use the method and it created dependency, would that not create dependency ?
not required since we are using constructor injection
The class name might be more specific other than that much generic. Since the overall class tries to onboard AVC subscriptions.
I think, we can create the dataspace if it does not exist even though this feature does not available in bash script. Then, this retry block would be unnecessary.

Returning an empty string will result in no-data model content persisted in the database having a file name related to it might not be a good approach. If the file content could not be read, then better to stop operation.

Also I am not sure how this code would be executed ??
onboardingRetries is initialized with 0.
if (onboardingRetries > 10) --> this will always be false.
so the control wont go inside and the counter cant increment.

NCMP dataspace is part of the set up and is expected to be there for NCMP , this class's responsibility is to only load the model in NCMP so if NCMP dataspace is not there then not to push to create the dataspace

don't think this should be inside teh api package
maybe create an ncmp.init or ncmp.boot package instead ?

there is no delay before you retry?
And instead of recusing I think you are better off having a simple loop inside this method which also mean that onboardingRetries can become a method variable
does this need to be public?

its OK to reuse the constants from the class you are testing...
Groovy can acces them even if they are private
Done -moved to ncmp.init on next patch  :)

I think its not needed anymore - (what does team thinks?) because in docker the application is not gonna start without the database system up , the loading of liquibase does not take a while plus the code is not ran until the application is set up and actually 'ready' which means that all initialisation before this is expected to have been set already
Done - no actually, private next patch -

in the try-catch block , if there is exception  - then the exception handler would catch it and then coz of "break" statement it will exit the loop.
So no more retries will happen.

Place the "break" statement after createAnchor method and then test it 😊
here im not recalling the method so the onboarding retries is not gonna be set to zero again, im not sure if waiting is necessary - see previous patch comment --> what does team think ?
NB: No need fo JavaDoc for private methods
NB: No need fo JavaDoc for private methods

NB: I meant just to use objectUnderTest.SUBSCRIPTION_DATASPACE_NAME where you need it, no need to redefine here
But it is a matter of preference not blocking comment
should we log a warning or even error in an else block?!

should NOT be testing internal variables it is NOT part of the behavior from a client point of view. And this might  prevent the solution evolving in the future!
You can only assert consequences and since this is a test using mocks maybe you cannot
Even assert logging is debatable...

I think you testign the positive scenario proving the service methdsd are called and the negative test taht assert teh retry mechanism is probaly good enough
its IS OKish to control a test using internal variabel but I stil wonder if it is really needed to do it this way...

handle exceptions here 
just, (mayeb exoit application)

Will it kill the Application Container and stop everything ??
have you tested this functionality ?
yeah it stops the application  - not the container itself.
I think we could do with an NcmpStartUpException or something like that? NcmpException is more to be used as a parent exception, its not used anywhere else

could imply yang model is invalid, would go with unable to read file instead, I think eeception could be thrown as well to prevent the empty string being returned and causing a more ambiguous exception being thrown later on

We need to disuss this at some stage.
Personally I woudn't take this extreme step of not starting the application coz we are unable to create schema-set/anchor when there are APIs which can be used to create schema-set and anchor using them.

But for now I will proceed looking at other part of the patch.
and why do we remove this ??
it's not used anywhere anymore. was used for the init container

Decision Made.
This is as expected.
